pattern: Sliding a running set of seen letters across the string while checking a modular condition against the running length turns a per-prefix definition into one linear pass.
hints: Grow the string one character at a time, keeping a running set of the distinct letters seen so far and the current prefix length.
At every prefix length, compare the size of that running set against the prefix length taken modulo 3.
Count a prefix whenever the two values are equal, and add up that count across every prefix length from 1 to the full length of the string.

edge_inputs.description: single character prefix, trivially one distinct letter
length exactly three, the first modulus wrap-around
length exactly one hundred, the maximum size
distinct count resets slower than length modulo 3, so most prefixes are not residues
prefix length that is a multiple of 3 can still qualify only if all 3 (or 3k) letters happen to be distinct in a way matching 0 mod 3, which never matches since distinct count is at least 1

stress.description: random lowercase string at the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: small alphabet (only 3 distinct letters) to stress the modulus condition
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
